prerequisites: A running Qdrant instance, either Qdrant Cloud or a local self-hosted deployment, to receive the embedded records.
For Qdrant Cloud, the cluster URL and a Qdrant API key (used by the indexing.auth_method.api_key field).
An account with API access for your chosen embedding provider such as OpenAI or Cohere, unless you extract embeddings from a field in the source records.
A target collection name and the distance metric to configure under the indexing settings.
Knowledge of which source fields to embed and which to store as metadata, used to fill the processing configuration.

authMethods.name: Qdrant API key (indexing)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate to a Qdrant Cloud cluster by supplying an API key under indexing.auth_method.api_key. Create the key in the Qdrant Cloud dashboard under the API Keys section of the Cluster Detail Page (Create, choose a name and optional expiration, optionally restrict it to specific collections, then copy the key, which is shown only once). For a local or self-hosted instance with no auth enabled, no API key is required.

authMethods.name: OpenAI embedding key
authMethods.summary: When using OpenAI embeddings, provide an OpenAI API key in embedding.openai_key so the connector can call the OpenAI embeddings API.

authMethods.name: Cohere embedding key
authMethods.summary: When using Cohere embeddings, provide a Cohere API key in embedding.cohere_key so the connector can call the Cohere embeddings API.

authMethods.name: Embedding provider key
authMethods.summary: Provide the embedding provider API key in embedding.api_key. Not needed when embeddings are extracted from a field in the source records.

setupSteps.title: Provision the Qdrant target
setupSteps.body: Stand up a Qdrant instance. For Qdrant Cloud, create a cluster and note its URL. For local mode, note the host, port, and gRPC port. Decide on the collection name and distance metric (Dot product, Cosine similarity, or Euclidean distance) you will use.

setupSteps.title: Create a Qdrant API key (cloud mode)
setupSteps.body: In the Qdrant Cloud dashboard, open the API Keys section on the Cluster Detail Page and click Create. Choose a name and an optional expiration, optionally restrict the key to specific collections, then create it and copy the key immediately, as it is shown only once. You will enter this value as indexing.auth_method.api_key.

setupSteps.title: Configure embedding
setupSteps.body: Set the required embedding configuration to match your chosen method. For OpenAI set embedding.openai_key, for Cohere set embedding.cohere_key, for another supported provider set embedding.api_key, or choose a field-based option that reads precomputed vectors from the source records (no key required).

setupSteps.title: Configure indexing
setupSteps.body: Fill the required indexing configuration: the Qdrant URL (cloud) or host and port (local), the auth method with the Qdrant API key under indexing.auth_method.api_key when applicable, the target collection, the distance metric, the Prefer gRPC option, and the payload field that holds the embedded text.

setupSteps.title: Configure processing and optional fields
setupSteps.body: Set the required processing configuration: the text fields to embed, the fields to store as metadata, and the text splitter (chunk size and overlap) settings. Optionally enable omit_raw_text to avoid storing the original text in the payload.
